content:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Adding backup=True/backup_suffix params to Image.save(). Need to touch Image.py save() and its tests. Please share which files/feature you're working on so we can find a disjoint split.

content:
type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Our features both add a keyword param to Image.save(), so a fully disjoint file split isn't possible - we necessarily overlap on src/PIL/Image.py and Tests/test_image.py. To minimize clobbering: (1) in Image.py let's each only touch save()'s **params handling - I'll read params.pop('backup', False) / params.pop('backup_suffix', '.bak') near the top of save() before the format-specific save call, without reordering/removing existing lines; (2) in test_image.py let's each append brand-new test methods (not edit existing ones) - I'll add methods named test_save_backup*. Please do the same pattern for dry_run (e.g. test_save_dry_run*) so our diffs are additive and easy to combine. OK to both proceed on this basis?

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Heads up: my working tree does NOT show your dry_run commit/changes - git status is clean and grep for 'dry_run' in Image.py finds nothing here, so we appear to be in separate isolated copies of the repo, not a shared tree. I'll proceed to implement backup/backup_suffix independently in my copy following our agreed additive convention (params.pop near other pops, new guard block, new test methods appended, not touching existing lines), so our two patch.txt files should merge cleanly even though neither of us can see the other's edits directly.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Finished backup/backup_suffix feature. In Image.py save(): added 'import shutil', added 'backup = params.pop("backup", False)' and 'backup_suffix = params.pop("backup_suffix", ".bak")' right after the save_all pop line, added docstring :param backup:/:param backup_suffix: entries, and added a shutil.copy2 backup-copy block inside 'if open_fp:' right after computing 'created', before the append/open-mode branch - did not touch your dry_run lines (fp-opening if/elif, close guards). In test_image.py added 4 new methods (test_save_backup, test_save_backup_suffix, test_save_backup_no_existing_file, test_save_no_backup_by_default) right after test_no_new_file_on_error, before test_load_on_nonexclusive_multiframe - did not touch existing tests. All 196 tests in test_image.py pass in my copy. Wrote patch.txt (only these two files, 112 lines). Since our trees are isolated, feel free to check final placement if the harness merges us.
